questions.question: What is the time complexity of inserting a new node at the beginning of a singly linked list?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of inserting a new node at the beginning of a singly linked list is O(1) because we only need to update the head pointer.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of deleting a node with a given value in a singly linked list?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of deleting a node with a given value in a singly linked list is O(n) because we need to traverse the list to find the node to be deleted.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of displaying the elements of a singly linked list?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of displaying the elements of a singly linked list is O(n) because we need to traverse the list to display all elements.

questions.question: What happens when you delete a node with only one child in a singly linked list?
options: The node is deleted and the child becomes the new head.
The child is deleted and the node becomes the new head.
questions.explanation: When you delete a node with only one child in a singly linked list, the child becomes the new head.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'next' pointer in a singly linked list?
options: To point to the next node in the list.
To point to the previous node in the list.
questions.explanation: The 'next' pointer in a singly linked list points to the next node in the list, allowing for efficient insertion and deletion of nodes.
